question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of caching in NetworkStatus() so that every call re-queries the DB via RetrieveGenesis(), RetrieveLatest(), and Entries(), causing repeated expensive queries that degrade shared DB performance and shut down >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: addressBookEntryRepo.Entries()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the latestNodeServiceEndpoints SQL query repeatedly via /network/status to cause table lock contention on address_book_entry, degrading the DB enough to shut down >= 30% of nodes that depend on it?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: addressBookEntryRepo.Entries()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fallback logic in Entries() that tries addressBook101 then addressBook102 to double the number of DB queries per /network/status call, amplifying DB load and causing >= 30% of nodes to become unavailable?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: addressBookEntryRepo.Entries()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the string_agg aggregation in the latestNodeServiceEndpoints SQL query to cause excessive memory allocation when the address book has many endpoints, crashing the DB and shutting down >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user use the node IP:port data from peers.ToRosetta() to identify nodes running on the same physical host and craft resource contention attacks (CPU/memory) that simultaneously affect >= 30% of co-located nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated /network/status endpoint to map the entire network topology via the Peers list and then use that map to identify critical routing nodes, targeting >= 30% of them for shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user use the complete list of errors.Errors exposed by /network/options to identify which errors are retriable (e.g., ErrDatabaseError code 125, ErrNodeIsStarting code 110) and craft requests that force nodes into infinite retry loops, consuming resources on >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the full OperationTypes list from /network/options to identify deprecated or less-tested operation types and craft malformed requests targeting those handlers on >= 30% of nodes, causing crashes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that /network/status returns the exact currentBlockTimestamp in milliseconds to synchronize a coordinated attack across multiple clients, all hitting >= 30% of nodes at the same consensus timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the IsOnline() check in NetworkStatus() by sending requests that cause the blockRepo to return nil-like states, tricking the service into returning ErrEndpointNotSupportedInOfflineMode and making >= 30% of nodes appear offline to other clients?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of request body size limits on /network/status (which accepts a *rTypes.NetworkRequest) to send oversized payloads that exhaust memory on the server, causing >= 30% of nodes to crash?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of request body size limits on /network/options (which accepts a *rTypes.NetworkRequest) to send oversized payloads that exhaust memory, causing >= 30% of nodes to become unavailable?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkList()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of request body size limits on /network/list (which accepts a *rTypes.MetadataRequest) to send oversized metadata payloads that exhaust memory, causing >= 30% of nodes to crash?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the node account IDs exposed in the Peers list from /network/status to identify specific node accounts and submit transactions that target those accounts, causing >= 30% of nodes to enter an error state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of genesisBlock and currentBlock data from /network/status to calculate the exact block production rate and use that to time attacks on >= 30% of nodes during block finalization windows?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: addressBookEntryRepo.Entries()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the coalesce(n.account_id, abe.node_account_id) logic in latestNodeServiceEndpoints to infer which nodes have overridden account IDs, identifying recently updated nodes that may be in a transitional state and targeting >= 30% of them?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated /network/status endpoint to continuously monitor the Peers list for changes, detecting when new nodes join and targeting them before they are fully initialized, causing >= 30% of nodes to be shut down?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that /network/status exposes all node endpoints without any filtering or masking to perform network reconnaissance and identify >= 30% of nodes that share a common infrastructure vulnerability?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the version information (RosettaVersion, NodeVersion) exposed by /network/options to identify nodes running vulnerable software versions and target >= 30% of them with version-specific exploits?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of IP-based rate limiting on /network/status to use distributed clients to flood the endpoint, causing the shared DB connection pool to be exhausted and shutting down >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the context propagation in NetworkStatus() by sending requests with very long deadlines to hold DB connections open indefinitely via addressBookEntryRepo.Entries(ctx), starving other nodes of DB connections and shutting down >= 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that /network/status returns the full peer list including node IDs to map which nodes are responsible for which account ranges, then target the nodes handling the most critical accounts to shut down >= 30% of processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: addressBookEntryRepo.Entries()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the join between address_book_entry and address_book_service_endpoint tables in latestNodeServiceEndpoints to cause a Cartesian product explosion when many endpoints exist, consuming excessive DB memory and shutting down >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of response size limits in /network/status to trigger responses with very large Peers lists, causing memory exhaustion in clients or intermediate proxies that serve >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential nature of RetrieveGenesis() followed by RetrieveLatest() in NetworkStatus() to cause a time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) race condition where the block state changes between calls, causing inconsistent responses that confuse >= 30% of nodes?
